---
title: "Collision EDA — STATS19"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
from pathlib import Path

from road_risk.config import _ROOT as ROOT

col = pd.read_parquet(ROOT / "data/processed/stats19/collision_clean.parquet")

# standardise year column name
year_col = "collision_year" if "collision_year" in col.columns else "accident_year"

print(f"Collisions: {len(col):,} rows | Years: {sorted(col[year_col].unique())} | Cols: {col.shape[1]}")
```

## Overview

This page explores the raw STATS19 collision records for the expanded study area 
(Northern and Central England) before any spatial joining or modelling. The goal is to
understand the coverage, quality, and structure of the outcome variable.

```{python}
by_year = col.groupby(year_col).size().reset_index(name="collisions")
by_sev = col.groupby([year_col, "collision_severity"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))

by_year.plot(x=year_col, y="collisions", ax=axes[0], legend=False, marker="o", color="steelblue")
axes[0].set_title("Total collisions by year")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Year")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Collisions")
axes[0].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
axes[0].grid(alpha=0.2)

if "collision_severity" in col.columns:
    by_sev.plot(kind="bar", ax=axes[1], stacked=True)
    axes[1].set_title("Collisions by severity and year")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Year")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Collisions")
    axes[1].tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)
    axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    axes[1].grid(alpha=0.2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.callout-note}
The sharp drop in 2020–2021 reflects COVID-19 lockdowns reducing traffic and
reported collisions. Both years are flagged `is_covid = True` and treated
separately where needed.
:::

---

## Severity distribution {#severity}

```{python}
if "collision_severity" in col.columns:
    sev = col["collision_severity"].value_counts().sort_index()
    labels = {1: "Fatal", 2: "Serious", 3: "Slight"}
    sev.index = sev.index.map(lambda x: labels.get(x, str(x)))

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
    bars = ax.bar(sev.index, sev.values, color=["#c0392b", "#e67e22", "#3498db"])
    for bar, val in zip(bars, sev.values):
        ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, bar.get_height() + 200,
                f"{val:,}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title("Collision severity distribution")
    ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    totals = sev.to_dict()
    for label, n in totals.items():
        pct = n / len(col) * 100
        print(f"  {label:8s}: {n:,} ({pct:.1f}%)")
```

---

## Temporal patterns {#temporal}

### By day of week

```{python}
if "day_of_week" in col.columns:
    dow_labels = {1: "Sun", 2: "Mon", 3: "Tue", 4: "Wed", 5: "Thu", 6: "Fri", 7: "Sat"}
    dow = col["day_of_week"].value_counts().sort_index()
    dow.index = dow.index.map(lambda x: dow_labels.get(x, str(x)))

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
    ax.bar(dow.index, dow.values, color="steelblue")
    ax.set_title("Collisions by day of week")
    ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### By hour of day

```{python}
if "hour" in col.columns:
    hour = col["hour"].value_counts().sort_index()

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
    ax.bar(hour.index, hour.values, color="steelblue", width=0.8)
    ax.set_title("Collisions by hour of day")
    ax.set_xlabel("Hour (24h)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
    ax.set_xticks(range(0, 24))
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### By month

```{python}
if "month" in col.columns:
    month_labels = {1:"Jan",2:"Feb",3:"Mar",4:"Apr",5:"May",6:"Jun",
                    7:"Jul",8:"Aug",9:"Sep",10:"Oct",11:"Nov",12:"Dec"}
    month = col["month"].value_counts().sort_index()
    month.index = month.index.map(lambda x: month_labels.get(x, str(x)))

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
    ax.bar(month.index, month.values, color="steelblue")
    ax.set_title("Collisions by month")
    ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

---

## Road context {#road-context}

### Road type and class

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))

if "road_type" in col.columns:
    rt = col["road_type"].value_counts().head(8)
    axes[0].barh(rt.index.astype(str), rt.values, color="steelblue")
    axes[0].set_title("Collisions by road type")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Collisions")
    axes[0].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    axes[0].invert_yaxis()
    axes[0].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

if "first_road_class" in col.columns:
    rc_labels = {1: "Motorway", 2: "A(M)", 3: "A road", 4: "B road", 5: "C road", 6: "Unclassified"}
    rc = col["first_road_class"].value_counts().sort_index()
    rc.index = rc.index.map(lambda x: rc_labels.get(x, str(x)))
    axes[1].bar(rc.index, rc.values, color="steelblue")
    axes[1].set_title("Collisions by first road class")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Collisions")
    axes[1].tick_params(axis="x", rotation=30)
    axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    axes[1].grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Speed limit

```{python}
if "speed_limit" in col.columns:
    sl = col[col["speed_limit"] > 0]["speed_limit"].value_counts().sort_index()

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
    ax.bar(sl.index.astype(str), sl.values, color="steelblue")
    ax.set_title("Collisions by speed limit (mph)")
    ax.set_xlabel("Speed limit (mph)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### Urban vs rural and junction context

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

if "urban_or_rural_area" in col.columns:
    ur = col["urban_or_rural_area"].map({1: "Urban", 2: "Rural"}).value_counts()
    axes[0].pie(ur.values, labels=ur.index, autopct="%1.1f%%",
                colors=["#3498db", "#27ae60"])
    axes[0].set_title("Urban vs rural split")

if "junction_detail" in col.columns:
    jd_labels = {
        0: "Not at junction", 1: "Roundabout", 2: "Mini-roundabout",
        3: "T/staggered junction", 5: "Slip road", 6: "Crossroads",
        7: "Multiple junction", 8: "Private drive", 9: "Other junction",
        99: "Unknown"
    }
    jd = col["junction_detail"].value_counts().sort_values(ascending=False).head(8)
    jd.index = jd.index.map(lambda x: jd_labels.get(x, str(x)))
    jd.plot(kind="barh", ax=axes[1], color="steelblue")
    axes[1].set_title("Collisions by junction type")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Collisions")
    axes[1].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    axes[1].invert_yaxis()
    axes[1].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Light conditions {#light}

```{python}
if "light_conditions" in col.columns:
    lc_labels = {
        1: "Daylight",
        4: "Dark: street lights",
        5: "Dark: no lights",
        6: "Dark: unknown lights",
        7: "Dark: lights unlit",
        -1: "Unknown"
    }
    lc = col["light_conditions"].value_counts().sort_values(ascending=False)
    lc.index = lc.index.map(lambda x: lc_labels.get(x, str(x)))

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))

    lc.plot(kind="barh", ax=axes[0], color="steelblue")
    axes[0].set_title("Collisions by light conditions")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Collisions")
    axes[0].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{x:,.0f}"))
    axes[0].invert_yaxis()
    axes[0].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    # Dark vs daylight severity breakdown
    col["_is_dark"] = col["light_conditions"].isin([4, 5, 6, 7])
    if "collision_severity" in col.columns:
        sev_labels = {1: "Fatal", 2: "Serious", 3: "Slight"}
        dark_sev = (
            col.groupby(["_is_dark", "collision_severity"])
            .size()
            .unstack(fill_value=0)
        )
        dark_sev.index = dark_sev.index.map({True: "Dark", False: "Daylight"})
        dark_sev.columns = dark_sev.columns.map(lambda x: sev_labels.get(x, str(x)))
        dark_sev_pct = dark_sev.div(dark_sev.sum(axis=1), axis=0) * 100
        dark_sev_pct.plot(kind="bar", stacked=True, ax=axes[1],
                          color=["#c0392b", "#e67e22", "#3498db"])
        axes[1].set_title("Severity split: daylight vs dark")
        axes[1].set_ylabel("Percentage of collisions")
        axes[1].tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0)
        axes[1].grid(axis="y", alpha=0.2)
        axes[1].legend(title="Severity")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    n_dark = col["_is_dark"].sum()
    print(f"  Dark collisions: {n_dark:,} ({n_dark/len(col):.1%} of total)")
```

---

## Coordinate quality {#coords}

```{python}
if "coords_valid" in col.columns:
    valid = col["coords_valid"].value_counts()
    suspect = col.get("coords_suspect", pd.Series(dtype=bool))

    print(f"  Coordinate quality:")
    print(f"    coords_valid=True   : {valid.get(True, 0):,}")
    print(f"    coords_valid=False  : {valid.get(False, 0):,}")
    if hasattr(suspect, "sum"):
        print(f"    coords_suspect=True : {suspect.sum():,} (>10 km from LSOA centroid)")

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
    if "lsoa_dist_m" in col.columns:
        dist = col["lsoa_dist_m"].dropna()
        dist_km = dist[dist < 20000] / 1000
        ax.hist(dist_km, bins=60, edgecolor="none", color="steelblue")
        ax.axvline(10, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label="10 km suspect threshold")
        ax.set_xlabel("Distance from LSOA centroid (km)")
        ax.set_ylabel("Collisions")
        ax.set_title("Collision distance from recorded LSOA centroid")
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(alpha=0.2)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
```

---

## Key observations

```{python}
total = len(col)
n_fatal = (col["collision_severity"] == 1).sum() if "collision_severity" in col.columns else None
n_dark = col["_is_dark"].sum() if "_is_dark" in col.columns else None
n_urban = (col["urban_or_rural_area"] == 1).sum() if "urban_or_rural_area" in col.columns else None
n_junction = (col["junction_detail"] == 0).sum() if "junction_detail" in col.columns else None

print(f"Total collisions analysed : {total:,}")
if n_fatal is not None:
    print(f"Fatal collisions          : {n_fatal:,} ({n_fatal/total:.1%})")
if n_dark is not None:
    print(f"Dark conditions           : {n_dark:,} ({n_dark/total:.1%})")
if n_urban is not None:
    print(f"Urban setting             : {n_urban:,} ({n_urban/total:.1%})")
if n_junction is not None:
    print(f"Not at junction           : {n_junction:,} ({n_junction/total:.1%})")
```

::: {.callout-note}
These raw proportions describe the collision dataset, not collision risk.
Risk requires dividing by exposure (vehicle-kilometres). Roads with high
urban or junction proportions may look dangerous here but score differently
after the exposure model is applied in Stage 2.
:::
